exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Goblet Squat
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your knee tracks in line with your second toe and your hip as you squat.

2. Step one foot at a time onto the flat side of the BOSU with your feet placed equal distances apart. Shift your bodyweight so you are centered with approximately half your bodyweight over each foot.
3.Lift the dumbbell to your chest. 

4.Lower yourself into a squat, keep your chest tall, upper arms horizontal to the ground while holding the weight at the top of your chest height in front of you.

5.Stand by extending through your knees, squeezing your glutes, and drawing abdominals in. 

6.Repeat prescribed number of repetitions.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Mountain Climber
exercises.description:
Place a BOSU ball dome-side down on the floor and get into a plank position with your hands on the flat side of the BOSU. 

Keep your body in a straight line and core engaged. Drive one knee toward your chest, then quickly switch legs, alternating knees as fast as possible while maintaining stability throughout the movement.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Mountain Climber to Push Up
exercises.description:
Place a BOSU ball dome-side down on the floor and get into a plank position with your hands on the flat side of the BOSU. 

Drive one knee toward your chest, then quickly switch legs, performing a mountain climber motion. 

After a set number of climbers or a continuous rhythm, perform a push-up while maintaining core stability.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Push Up
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain tight abdominals and active glutes. This will maintain neutral spine throughout the push up.

2) From kneeling, place your hands equal distance apart, and centered on the BOSU.

3) Engage your abdominals and glutes. Gradually put an equal amount of body weight over your hands as you extend your legs out behind you one at a time. 

4) Move your body to make a straight line along your shoulders, neutral spine, hips, knees, and ankles. Ensure your shoulders are directly above your hands.
 
5) Once in the top of a push-up position, lower yourself to the BOSU while maintaining a neutral spine.

6) Repeat as prescribed.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Push-Up with Knee Tuck
exercises.alternateName: BOSU Dome Down Push-Up with slow climber
exercises.description:
Place a BOSU ball dome-side down on the floor and get into a plank position with your hands on the flat side of the BOSU. 


Keep your body in a straight line and core engaged. Perform a push-up, then immediately tuck your knees toward your chest while keeping your feet on the floor. 

Extend your legs back to plank and repeat continuously.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Squat
exercises.alternateName: Upside Down Body Weight Bosu Squat
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your knees track in line with your second toe and your hips.

2.You’ll also want to squeeze your shoulder blades together lightly at the bottom of the squat to help maintain nice posture through the upper body.

3.Tilt the BOSU to one side.  Step on it with one foot, and then place the other foot on the BOSU an equal distance from the center as the other foot. Balance your bodyweight evenly between your two feet. 

4.Slowly squat down. Your arms can reach in front of you to counter balance your hips sitting back. Stop at a depth where you can maintain form.

5.Return to the start position by standing.

6.Repeat for the number of reps.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Step Up
exercises.alternateName: BOSU Up Side Down Single Leg Body Weight Step Up
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure you abdominals are engaged throughout the whole exercise. This will help you balance.

2. With the BOSU placed dome side down, place one foot centered on top with the other foot off the BOSU on the ground.

3.Stand on one leg by loading your bodyweight over top of the foot on the dome of the BOSU by squeezing your glutes, leaning forward, and extending through the front leg until you are standing tall on one leg. 

4.Bring your free leg's knee up in front of you, and counter check your torso's rotation by bringing the opposite arm (opposite from your free leg) across your body. 

5.Return to the start position by lowering yourself to the ground, sitting and hinging your hips back, and reaching with the free leg to the ground.

6.When you’ve completed one side, repeat the last part of the set on the other side.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Alternating Push Up
exercises.alternateName: BOSU Dome Up shuffle Push Up
exercises.description:
Start in a high plank with one hand on the dome side of the BOSU and the other hand on the floor beside it. 

Lower your chest into a push-up, then push through your hands to return to plank. Next, walk your hands over the BOSU, switching positions so the opposite hand is now on the dome and the other on the floor. 

Repeat the push-up and continue walking over, alternating hands with each rep.

exercises.name: Bosu Dome Up Alternating Push Ups
exercises.description:
Place the BOSU dome-side up. Get into a high plank position with hands on the dome. 

Lower your chest into a push-up, then push back up. Alternate hand positions on and off the BOSU dome.

exercises.name: Bosu Dome up Alternating Step Ups
exercises.description:
Stand in front of the BOSU dome-side up. Step one foot onto the dome, then bring the other foot up to balance. 

Step back down and alternate starting legs. Maintain control throughout.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Bicycle Crunch
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure active abdominals throughout. To do so, make sure that you have a shorten distance between the bottom of your ribs and the top of your hips at the front of your body.

2) On one side of your body, bring your knee towards the opposite elbow, whilst extending the other leg; simultaneously twist your body to bring your elbow towards the opposing bending knee, while turning your body and the other elbow away. Maintain a slight crunch during this.  

3) Alternate by switching leg, body, and arm positions. 

4) Perform a certain number of repetitions.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Bird Dog
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain active abdominals. This will help you keep a stable quiet body as you alternate opposing arm and leg throughout the movement.

2) Center your knees on the top BOSU close together. Set your hands directly under your shoulders so your arms are perpendicular to the ground. 

3) Set your abdominals. Lift one leg and the opposite arm simultaneously, while keeping your torso square to the ground.

4) Move your arm and leg until they are in line with your body (horizontal to the ground) in a straight line.

5) Return to the start position, and switch the leg and arm for the next rep.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Clap Push Up
exercises.description:
Start in a high plank position with your hands on the dome side of the BOSU and feet hip-width apart. 

Lower your chest toward the BOSU, then explosively push through your hands to lift your body off the BOSU. 

While in the air, quickly clap your hands together before landing back onto the dome in the starting plank position.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Clapping Push Up
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain active abdominals and glutes throughout. This will help you keep neutral spine.

2) You’ll also want to ensure that when you’re landing BOSU, your hands are roughly equal distance from the center line.

3) From kneeling, place your hands equal distance apart, and centered on the dome side of the BOSU.

4) Engage your abdominals and glutes. Extend your legs out behind you one at a time. 

5) Move your body to make a straight line along your shoulders, neutral spine, hips, knees, and ankles. Ensure your shoulders are directly above your hands. 

6) Once in the top of a push-up position, lower yourself to the BOSU while maintaining a neutral spine. 

7) Push up off the BOSU, with your upper body taking flight and clap mid-air, all whilst maintaining a neutral spine and linear body position. 

8) Make contact with the BOSU and progressively decelerate as you lower yourself into the bottom position of a push-up. 

9) Begin your next explosive push up as soon as you reach the bottom of your push-up position.

10) Repeat as prescribed.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Crunch
exercises.description:
1) Sit on the front of the dome of the BOSU. Place your hands across your chest (easier), or place your fingertips by your ears (harder). Feet will stay on the ground throughout the exercise.

2) Roll back, laying out along the top of the BOSU while maintaining active abdominals.   

3) Breathe out and roll back up to a crunched position (rather than sitting all the way up). 

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Dumbbell Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll end up bridging your hips up so that means glutes active and abdominals on.

2) From there, you’ll fly out with the dumbbells. You’ll want to ensure they stay in line with your chest and that you have a slight elbow bend.

3) Don’t bend your elbows too much, just 5-degree bend is enough.

4) From there, make sure that you pull away back to the center line by squeezing your chest.

5) Perform certain number of repetitions.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Dumbbell Chest Press
exercises.description:
1) Lay on the BOSU's dome with dumbbells in each hand. Feet under your knees, and hips on the ground. 

2) Set your abdominals and squeeze your glutes to lift your hips off the ground, whilst lifting the dumbbells close to your body and raising them above your chest. 

3) Lower the weights by bending your elbows at 90-degree angles so that your forearms are perpendicular to the ground and over your elbows. Press the weights upward until the two weights meet at the top over your chest. 

4) Repeat until rep range or failure is met.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Dumbbell Goblet Squat
exercises.alternateName: BOSU Right Side Up Dumbbell Goblet Squat
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your knees are track in the second toe and your hip as you go into a squat.

2.You’ll also want to keep your upper body set  and hold the dumbbell high the lower into the squat you go.

3. Step one foot at a time onto the dome side of the BOSU with your feet placed equal distances apart. Shift your bodyweight so you are centered with approximately half your bodyweight over each foot. Lift the dumbbell to your chest.

4. Lower yourself into a squat. Keep your chest tall, elbows horizontal to the ground while holding the weight at the top of your chest height in front of you. 

5.Stand by extending through your knees, squeezing your glutes, and drawing abdominals in. Minimize wobbles throughout this portion of the exercise as well. 

6. Repeat prescribed number of repetitions.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Dumbbell Squat
exercises.alternateName: BOSU Right Side Up Double Dumbbell Hang Suitcase Squat
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that your knees track over top of your second toe and in line with your hip.

2. From there, you’ll also want to slightly squeeze your shoulder blades as you get lower into the squat.

3. Step one foot at a time onto the dome side of the BOSU with your feet placed equal distances apart. Shift your bodyweight so you are cent red with approximately half your bodyweight over each foot. Have dumbbells in hand.

4. Lower yourself into a squat, keeping knees in line with your second toe and a neutral or a slight spinal backward bend.

5.Stand by extending through your knees, squeezing your glutes, and drawing abdominals in.

6.Repeat prescribed number of repetitions, or for prescribed amount of time.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Dumbbell Squat and Press
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, maintain an active core throughout. During the squat portion, you’ll want to maintain proper squat form which means knees in line with your toes and hips.

2) When you press up overhead, ensure that your arm is straightened directly up towards the ceiling having your biceps come in line with your ears.

3) Step one foot at a time onto the dome side of the BOSU with your feet placed equal distances apart. Lift the dumbbells to your shoulder height. 

4) Lower yourself into a squat, keeping knees in line with your second toe and hip and light spinal back bend (extension). 

5) Stand up from your squat, activating your glutes and drawing your abdominals in as you stand. 

6) Once in neutral spine, press the dumbbells overhead fully with your arms touching your ears and arms straight. Then, bring the weights back to your shoulders.

7) Perform certain number of repetitions.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Dumbbell Thruster
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain an active core throughout. 

2) When you do your squat, perform a proper squat form which means knees tracking in line with your second toe and your hips.

3) Step one foot at a time onto the dome side of the BOSU with your feet placed equal distances apart. Lift the dumbbells to your shoulder height. 

4) Lower yourself into a squat.

5) In one fluid movement both stand up from your squat and press the dumbbells overhead fully with your arms touching your ears and arms straight. 

6) Then, in one movement, bring the weights back to your shoulders while squatting again.

7) Repeat prescribed number of repetitions.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Elbow Side Plank
exercises.alternateName: Upright BOSU Half Side Core Plank
exercises.description:
(1) Lie on your side and place one elbow and forearm centred on the top of the round part of the BOSU. Stack your feet on top of each other, or lay them next to each other(one in front of the other). 

(2) Lift your hips, squeezing your glutes, extending your legs, and drawing abs in engaging them. Ensure your shoulder is over top of your elbow. Hold this neutral spine position (shoulders, spine, hips, knees, ankles all in line) actively. 

(3) Perform the provided repetitions, or holds until prescribed directions are completed. 

*Alternatively you can perform the dynamic side plank.  Lower your hips until they touch the floor and raise them back to the original position and repeat.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Glute Bridge
exercises.description:
1) Lie on the floor with the BOSU ball at your feet. Place your feet on the top of the BOSU ball.  Your knees should be directly above your ankles. The round side of the BOSU can be facing up, or you can have the ball upside down. To have the ball upside down makes the exercise more difficult. 

2) Draw in your abdominals as you lift your hips up towards the ceiling by primarily using our glutes to lift. Make sure not to arch and lift excessively from your low back. Hold briefly at the top of the lift, squeezing your glutes. 

3) Slowly return to the mat. 

4) Repeat either with holds, or reps, until prescribed exercise is complete.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Push Up
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure your abdominals and glutes are engaged to maintain neutral spine throughout your push up. 

2) You’ll also want to place your hands equal distance apart from one another on the BOSU ball.

3) From kneeling, place your hands equal distance apart and center on the BOSU.

4) Engage your abdominals and glutes. Gradually put an equal amount of body weight over your hands as you extend your legs out behind you one at a time. 

5) Move your body to make a straight line along your shoulders, neutral spine, hips, knees, and ankles. Ensure your shoulders are directly above your hands. 

6) Once in the top of a push-up position, lower yourself to the BOSU while maintaining a neutral spine. Keep your elbows over top of your hands, and in line with your chest. 

7) Push up to the starting top position, while maintaining neutral spine and body position.

8) Repeat as prescribed.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Scissors
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to maintain active abdominals. To do so, shorten the distance between the bottom of your ribs and the top of your hips.

2) Place your sit bones (tailbone and bottom of the pelvis) at the peak of the BOSU dome. Lean back to the point where you can balance on your sit bones with your legs in the air. 

3) While maintaining slight forward flexion through your lumbar spine, begin to alternate kicking with straight legs.

4) Repeat for the prescribed allotment of time, or repetitions.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Side Lunge
exercises.alternateName: Unilateral Single Side BOSU Right Side Up Side Lunge Hip Hinge
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure, your hip, knee and second toe line up on the side that you’re lunging towards.

2) Place one foot in the top center of the dome of the BOSU. The other foot should be placed wider than shoulder width apart on the ground beside the BOSU.
 
3) Load the foot on top of the BOSU by leaning your body weight over top of the BOSU, hinging at the hip. Press through the leg on the ground fully extending it.
 
4) Return to the start position by straightening the leg that is on top of the BOSU, squeezing your glutes, quads, and drawing abs in. 

5) Repeat until your prescribed reps.

6) Once you’ve completed one side, do the other side to finish the set.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Single Arm Renegade Row
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’re gonna maintain a single arm plank. While doing so, keep the glutes active as well as the abdominals engaged.

2) Kneel in front of the BOSU, place your hand in the center of the dome. 

3) Engage your abdominals and glutes to maintain a neutral spine as you load your arm up with your body weight. Ensure your shoulder is above and in line with your hand on the BOSU ball. 

4) While in the single arm plank position, take hold of the dumbbell with your free hand.

5) Pull the dumbbell towards your body, not beyond it. Return to the start position. 

6) Once you’ve completed one side, repeat the next part of the set with the other arm.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Staggered Push Up
exercises.alternateName: Offset bosu push up single arm
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure that your abdominals and glutes are active to maintain neutral spine throughout.

2) From there, you’ll also want to have your hands placed wide enough that at the bottom of the push up, your elbows stay over top of your hands.

3)Position a single BOSU to one side.  Put one hand on the BOSU and the other hand on the floor. 

4) Slowly lower your chest towards the floor and push yourself back up.  Keep your head and spine in neutral alignment. 

5) Perform a certain number of repetitions.

6) Once you’ve done a set on one side, repeat it on the other.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Up Step Up
exercises.alternateName: BOSU Right Side Up Single Leg Stand One Side
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure your abdominals are engaged the whole time both while stepping up on the BOSU  and lowering yourself down.

2) With the BOSU placed dome side up, place one foot centered on top with the other foot off the BOSU on the ground.

3) Stand on one leg by loading your bodyweight over top of the foot on the dome of the BOSU by squeezing your glutes, leaning forward, and extending through the front leg until you are standing tall on one leg. 

4) Bring your free leg's knee up in front of you, and counter check your torso's rotation by bringing the opposite arm (opposite from your free leg) across your body. Keep your abdominals drawn in, glutes active, and active leg to minimize wobbles and maintain balance. 

5) Return to the start position by lowering yourself to the ground, sitting and hinging your hips back, and reaching with the free leg to the ground.

6) Once you’ve completed one side, repeat it on the other.

exercises.name: BOSU Full Side Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure that you have a straight line throughout your body. To do so, engage your glutes to tack your hips and get your abdominals active. 

2) You’ll also want to aim to have your hand directly underneath your shoulder.

3) Lie on your side and place one hand centered on the top of the round part of the BOSU.  Stack your feet on top of each other.

4) Lift your hips, squeezing your glutes, extending your legs, and drawing abs in (engaging them). Ensure your shoulder is over top of your elbow. Hold this neutral spine position.

5) When you’ve completed one side, repeat it on the other.

exercises.name: BOSU Lateral Bounce
exercises.description:
Stand beside a BOSU ball with the dome side up. Jump laterally, landing one foot on the dome of the BOSU while the other leg is lifted off the floor. 

Immediately push off the BOSU to jump to the other side or back to the starting side, keeping your core engaged and knees slightly bent throughout. Repeat continuously.

exercises.name: Bosu Lateral Bounce to Squat Jump
exercises.description:
Stand beside a BOSU ball with the dome side up. Jump laterally, landing one foot on the BOSU while keeping the other leg off the floor. 

Push off the BOSU and land on one leg first on the floor, then immediately drop into a squat and explode upward into a squat jump.

exercises.name: BOSU Lunge to Balance
exercises.description:
Start in a reverse lunge position with your front foot on the dome of a BOSU ball and your rear foot on the floor. 

Push through your front heel to rise up, driving your back knee forward toward your chest into a balance position. 

Lower your leg back to the starting reverse lunge position and repeat, maintaining balance and control throughout the movement.

exercises.name: BOSU Plank to Pike
exercises.description:
Place a BOSU ball dome-side down on the floor and get into a plank position with your elbows on the flat side of the BOSU and your body in a straight line from head to heels. 

Engage your core and lift your hips toward the ceiling, forming a pike position. Lower your hips back down into the plank and repeat continuously, keeping your shoulders stable and core tight throughout.

exercises.name: BOSU Reverse Lunge with Knee Drive
exercises.description: Start standing on the dome of a BOSU ball with both feet. Step one foot backward off the BOSU into a reverse lunge while keeping your balance, then push through your front foot (on the BOSU) to rise up and drive your rear knee forward toward your chest in a knee-drive.

exercises.name: Bosu running man
exercises.description: If the Bosu is too difficult try doing this on the floor or fold a mat and stand on that. Opposite arm touches opposite knee. Whatever leg you are standing on is the arm that starts in with the other knee. It take a few tries to get the feel.

exercises.name: BOSU Single Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to use your glutes to lift your hips in the air. You’ll also want to engage your core. Doing so will help ensure that your hips don’t drop out on the side that isn’t supported by a leg.

2) Lie on the floor with the BOSU ball at your feet. Place one foot centered on top of the BOSU ball. Your knee should be directly above your ankle. Keep your free leg in the air, with a slight knee bend if needed.

3) Draw in your abdominals as you lift your hips up towards the ceiling by primarily using our glutes to lift. Hold briefly at the top of the lift, squeezing your glutes. 

4) Slowly lower your hips to the start position. Repeat rep range on the one side until prescribed reps are complete. 

5) When you’re done one side, do the other.
